title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 12: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What number do you get when you add 23 and 19?
Mia drew a closed shape made of 4 equal straight edges meeting at right angles. What is the name of her figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='15' width='50' height='50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with four equal sides.
questions.solution: A closed figure with 4 equal sides and right angles is a square.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 and lasts 45 minutes. What time does the movie end?
Look at the sequence: 2, 5, 8, 11, ? What number comes next?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 3, so 11 + 3 = 14.

questions.question_text: Leo has one coin of 10 cents, two coins of 5 cents, and three coins of 1 cent. How many cents does he have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='14'/><text x='20' y='25' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11'>10</text><circle cx='55' cy='20' r='12'/><text x='55' y='25' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>5</text><circle cx='85' cy='20' r='12'/><text x='85' y='25' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>5</text><circle cx='115' cy='20' r='10'/><text x='115' y='24' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>1</text><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><text x='140' y='24' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>1</text><circle cx='165' cy='20' r='10'/><text x='165' y='24' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>1</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Six coins lined up: one 10-cent, two 5-cent, and three 1-cent coins.

questions.question_text: A pencil is 14 cm long. How many millimeters is that?
questions.solution: 1 cm = 10 mm, so 14 cm = 140 mm.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Emma eats 2 slices. What fraction of the whole pizza did Emma eat?
questions.image_description: A pizza divided into 8 equal slices.
questions.solution: 2 out of 8 slices equals 2/8, which simplifies to 1/4.

questions.question_text: Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Cara. Who is the shortest among them?
questions.solution: Since Anna > Ben > Cara, Cara is the shortest.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 27. What is the largest of these numbers?
questions.solution: The numbers are 8, 9, 10, summing to 27. The largest is 10.

questions.question_text: How many small unit squares of equal size are needed to fully cover the rectangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='100' height='40'/><line x1='35' y1='10' x2='35' y2='50'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='50'/><line x1='85' y1='10' x2='85' y2='50'/><line x1='10' y1='30' x2='110' y2='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into a 4 by 2 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: The rectangle is 4 columns by 2 rows, so 4 x 2 = 8 squares.

questions.question_text: A garland repeats the four-bead pattern red, red, blue, green. What color is the 15th bead from the start?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='15' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>R</text><circle cx='40' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='40' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>R</text><circle cx='65' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='65' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>B</text><circle cx='90' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='90' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>G</text><circle cx='115' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='115' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>R</text><circle cx='140' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='140' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>R</text><circle cx='165' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='165' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>B</text><circle cx='190' cy='15' r='8'/><text x='190' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>G</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A garland showing two repeats of R, R, B, G.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 4 beads. 15 divided by 4 gives remainder 3, so the 15th bead matches position 3 in the pattern, which is blue.

questions.question_text: Sam buys a notebook for $3 and two pens that cost $2 each. He pays with a $10 bill. How much change does he receive?
questions.solution: Total cost = 3 + 2 + 2 = $7. Change = 10 - 7 = $3.

questions.question_text: The clock currently reads 8:40. What time will it be 1 hour and 35 minutes later?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='32'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='18'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='20' y2='40'/><text x='40' y='16' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8'>12</text><text x='65' y='44' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8'>3</text><text x='40' y='72' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8'>6</text><text x='15' y='44' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8'>9</text></svg>

questions.question_text: In a group of 5 friends, every person shakes hands exactly once with every other person. How many handshakes happen in total?
questions.solution: Number of handshakes = 5 x 4 / 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: A garden bed is 5 meters long and 3 meters wide. What is the distance all the way around the garden bed?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='20' width='90' height='40'/><text x='60' y='15' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>5 m</text><text x='112' y='42' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='start' font-size='10'>3 m</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 5 m by 3 m.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2 x (5 + 3) = 16 m.

questions.question_text: Which number makes this equation true: 6 times ? minus 5 equals 37 ?
Tom, Sara, Max, and Jen sit in a row of four seats. Max is at the far left. Tom is not at either end. Sara is immediately to the right of Jen. Who sits at the far right end?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='145' y='10' width='40' height='25'/><text x='30' y='27' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>1</text><text x='75' y='27' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>2</text><text x='120' y='27' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>3</text><text x='165' y='27' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four empty seats numbered 1 through 4 in a row.
questions.solution: Max is at seat 1. Tom must be at seat 2 or 3. Jen and Sara are next to each other with Sara on Jen's right, so they must be at seats 2-3 or 3-4. Combined with Tom at seat 2 or 3, the only valid arrangement is Max(1), Tom(2), Jen(3), Sara(4). So Sara sits at the far right.

questions.question_text: A rectangle measuring 6 cm by 4 cm is cut into two pieces along a straight line from one corner to the opposite corner. What is the area of each of the two resulting triangles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='40'/><line x1='10' y1='10' x2='70' y2='50'/><text x='40' y='7' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9'>6</text><text x='74' y='32' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' text-anchor='start' font-size='9'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A 6 by 4 rectangle split by its diagonal into two triangles.
questions.solution: Rectangle area = 6 x 4 = 24. Each triangle has half that area: 24 / 2 = 12 square centimeters.

questions.question_text: The product of two whole numbers is 48 and their sum is 14. What is the larger of the two numbers?
questions.solution: Factor pairs of 48 include 6 and 8, which sum to 14. The larger is 8.

questions.question_text: Start with the number 1. On each step, either add 2 or multiply by 2. Using exactly three steps, what is the greatest number that can be reached?
questions.solution: Try 1 -> 3 (+2) -> 6 (x2) -> 12 (x2). Other sequences give smaller results, so the maximum is 12.

questions.question_text: A chocolate bar is broken into 12 equal pieces. Lily eats 1/3 of the bar and Kai eats 1/4 of the bar. How many pieces are left?
questions.image_description: A long chocolate bar divided into 12 equal pieces.
questions.solution: 1/3 of 12 = 4 pieces; 1/4 of 12 = 3 pieces. Eaten total = 7. Left = 12 - 7 = 5.

questions.question_text: A jug holds 2 liters when full. A small glass holds 250 milliliters. How many small glasses can be completely filled from one full jug?
questions.solution: 2 liters = 2000 mL. 2000 / 250 = 8 glasses.

questions.question_text: A box contains 5 red balls, 4 blue balls, and 3 green balls. Without looking, what is the smallest number of balls that must be taken out to be sure of having at least two balls of the same color?
questions.solution: Three balls could be all different colors (one of each). The fourth ball must repeat a color, guaranteeing a matching pair.

questions.question_text: A triangle, a square, and a circle are drawn side by side. If the circle is considered to have no corners, how many corners (vertices) do the three figures have altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,50 35,15 55,50'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><circle cx='140' cy='32' r='18'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle, a square, and a circle drawn side by side.
questions.solution: Triangle has 3 vertices, square has 4, circle has 0. Total = 3 + 4 + 0 = 7.
